claim_stress_audit.detail: Any near-100 repair language must define repair recovery ratio.
No broad manipulation-solved wording is allowed.

claim_stress_audit.check: does not claim repairs always fix EBMs
claim_stress_audit.detail: Repairs are reported as measured artifacts, not guarantees.

claim_stress_audit.check: optional success claim blocked when zero
claim_stress_audit.detail: Optional-suite success can be claimed only when generated artifacts report nonzero success.

claim_stress_audit.check: autonomous Meta-World claim blocked without low-dependency success
claim_stress_audit.detail: Autonomous Meta-World success requires the ungated non-expert-centered threshold artifact.

claim_stress_audit.check: autonomous learned-policy claim blocked without learned threshold
claim_stress_audit.detail: Autonomous learned-policy success requires a learned no-gate non-expert-centered threshold artifact.

claims.claim: The finite tie-aware tail selection law predicts selected real utility for a fixed score/utility pool.
Controlled contact and multimodal toys exhibit low-energy tail miscalibration: selected energy decreases while real utility falls.
A small learned contrastive EBM can over-optimize an observation-limited low-energy tail, and calibrated/value-shaped scoring improves high-N utility.
A PyTorch MLP IBC-style EBM trains with decreasing contrastive loss and produces finite exact-law selected-utility curves.
Meta-World reach-v3, push-v3, pick-place-v3, and button-press-v3 artifacts train the primary benchmark EBM on scripted expert actions; high-reward sampled actions are only an ablation.

A CPU-feasible closed-loop dependency audit reports gated, ungated, expert-centered, learned-demo-proposal, behavior-cloned-proposal, state-heuristic, local, random, and scripted variants without scripted continuation.
In the closed-loop Meta-World audit, the state-heuristic proposal variants clear the no-gate, no-runtime-scripted-expert low-dependency success threshold.
Energy reliability diagrams bucket candidates by energy quantile and expose the extreme low-energy tail.
A tail-aligned EBM can represent two valid modes better than explicit MSE regression, while a shortcut energy can still fail at high N.
More EBM inference compute trades energy evaluations and latency proxy against real utility, and added candidates can over-optimize energy.
Support penalties, conservative stopping, pilot-label calibration, value shaping, and oracle scoring form a repair hierarchy for selected-tail utility.
On supported local repair stress tests, the main pilot-label combined repair nearly closes the measured high-N gap by the repair recovery ratio definition.
The paper-facing figures, including repair recovery and attack-surface audit figures, are regenerated from local CSV/JSON artifacts.
Optional non-Meta-World adapters produce finite guarded rollout artifacts; reward and success are reported honestly and do not support broad manipulation success.
At least one optional non-Meta-World suite reports nonzero scripted-policy success while remaining scoped to finite simulator rollouts.
Forbidden clone and overclaim statements are treated as disallowed, not supported findings.

non_claim_boundaries.evidence: No real-robot artifacts are present; docs and claim stress audit block real-robot validation wording.
State-heuristic low-dependency success is supported, but nearest-demo, behavior-cloned, and local learned variants do not clear the learned-policy threshold.
All simulator claims remain artifact-scoped and do not support broad manipulation or real-robot success.

not_clone_audit.check: raw EBM compared to calibrated/value-shaped EBM
no real-robot validation claim in supported claims
does not imply EBMs always work
does not imply tail selection always helps
near-complete repair wording defines recovery ratio
does not duplicate WAM/JEPA/diffusion failure wording as the EBM claim
